ListItem.Para: Inoperative VFSG is connected.
Verify ELEC GEN DRIVE is not displayed when associated engine is running.
Verify the associated GEN CTRL indication box shows an amber “X” on the electrical synoptic page.
Verify the associated Electrical Panel (P5) drive light is illuminated while engine is off.
Both Left and Right ASG generators operate normally.
Left APU generator control unit operates normally.
APU battery operates normally.
APU battery charger operates normally.
APU is started before departure and operated continuously throughout the flight.
Flight remains within 180 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.
Repairs are made within three flight days.

Note.Para: For ELEC GEN SYS L1 or R1 status message, right ram air fan motor controller and associated right ram air fan must operate normally (RAM FAN CONTROL R and RAM FAN R are not displayed) for main engine start.

For ELEC GEN SYS L2 or R2 status message, left ram air fan motor controller and associated left ram air fan must operate normally (RAM FAN CONTROL L and RAM FAN L are not displayed) for main engine start.
For one or two CAC also inoperative (MEL item 21-51-01), one or two specific VFSGs must be operative.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Leave the inoperative VFSG connected (AMM DDG 24-22-01).

ListItem.Para: Set or verify the associated Electrical Panel (P5) GEN CTRL switch is ON.

Note.rev:revHighlight: Added note to specify switch must remain ON for further clarification.
Note.Para: The GEN CTRL switch must remain in the ON position.

ListItem.Para: Verify the associated Electrical Panel (P5) drive light is illuminated while engine is off.
Start the associated engine.

Note.Para: Steps 3 through 5 may be accomplished based on Aircraft Maintenance Record/Logbook of prior flight segment or engine run.

ListItem.Para: Verify the associated GEN CTRL indication box shows an amber "X" on the electrical synoptic page
Verify the associated ELEC GEN DRIVE advisory message is not displayed.

Note.Para: Expect engine start time to be longer.
For in-flight engine restart attempts above FL 250, use windmilling start envelope and procedures. Restart envelope and procedures below FL 250 are unchanged.
Single engine start is recommended.

Note.rev:revHighlight: Added additional note to provide clarity to flight crews on when the MMEL verification can occur, but also provides a step for the flight crew to perform the required verification, if desired.
Note.Para: After engine start, the associated GEN CTRL indication box shows an amber X on the electrical synoptic page.

ListItem.Para: The flight must remain within 180 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.

Increase flight planning fuel by either using an airplane drag correction factor of 1.0% or increase the flight planning fuel by 1.2% with APU running to account for the open APU air inlet door fully open.
Increase flight planning fuel by 250 lb (114 kg) per hour of APU usage inflight and 480 lb (218 kg) per hour of APU usage on the ground.
Start the APU before departure and operate the APU continuously.

Para.rev:revHighlight: MISC SYSTEM CTRLS page does not provide the necessary data to confirm the ram air fan is operating.

ListItem.Para: For VFSG L1 or R1 inoperative, On MISC SYSTEM CTRLS (MSC) maintenance page, confirm right ram fan is operating:
Select "SYS" switch on the Display Select Panel to display the SYS page on an MFD.
Select the MAINT button on the SYS menu.
Select the DISPLAY button for "21 AIR CONDITIONING".
On AIR CONDITIONING maintenance page 2, confirm RAM FAN SPEED KRPM under R is 6.0 or greater.
For VFSG L2 or R2 inoperative, On MISC SYSTEM CTRLS (MSC) maintenance page, confirm left ram fan is operating:
Select "SYS" switch on the Display Select Panel to display the SYS page on an MFD.
Select the MAINT button on the SYS menu.
Select the DISPLAY button for "21 AIR CONDITIONING".
On AIR CONDITIONING maintenance page 2, confirm RAM FAN. SPEED KRPM under L is 6.0 or greater.
ELEC GEN OFF XX advisory message will be displayed while engine is running.
ELEC ENGINE STARTER Lx or Rx may be displayed after engine start.
Inoperative VFSG is disconnected.
Both Left and Right ASG generators operate normally.
Left APU generator control unit operates normally.
APU battery operates normally.
APU battery charger operates normally.
APU is started before departure and operated continuously throughout the flight.
Flight remains within 180 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.
Repairs are made within three flight days.

Note.Para: For VFSG L1, L2, or R2 disconnected, the PFCS INTERFACE status message will be displayed. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 27-02-03.

For ELEC GEN SYS L1 or R1 status message, right ram air fan motor controller and associated right ram air fan must operate normally (RAM FAN CONTROL R and RAM FAN R are not displayed) for main engine start.

For ELEC GEN SYS L2 or R2 status message, left ram air fan motor controller and associated left ram air fan must operate normally (RAM FAN CONTROL L and RAM FAN L are not displayed) for main engine start.
For one or two CAC also inoperative (MEL item 21-51-01), one or two specific VFSGs must be operative.

ListItem.Para: Start the associated engine.

Note.Para: Steps 1 through 3 may be accomplished based on Aircraft Maintenance Record/Logbook of prior flight segment or engine run.

ListItem.Para: Disconnect the VFSG using the associated generator DRIVE disconnect switch.

Note.Para: Operating the airplane with a disconnected VFSG will cause internal heat damage to the VFSG input shaft and disconnect mechanism.

ListItem.Para: On the Electrical Synoptic page, confirm the associated green GEN DRIVE indication box shows an amber “X”.

Note.Para: Expect engine start time to be longer.
For in-flight engine restart attempts above FL 250, use windmilling start envelope and procedures. Restart envelope and procedures below FL 250 are unchanged.
Single engine start is recommended.

ListItem.Para: The flight must remain within 180 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.

Increase flight planning fuel by either using an airplane drag correction factor of 1.0% or increase the flight planning fuel by 1.2% with APU running to account for the open APU air inlet door fully open.
Increase flight planning fuel by 250 lb (114 kg) per hour of APU usage inflight and 480 lb (218 kg) per hour of APU usage on the ground.
Start the APU before departure and operate the APU continuously.

Para.rev:revHighlight: MISC SYSTEM CTRLS page does not provide the necessary data to confirm the ram air fan is operating.

ListItem.Para: For VFSG L1 or R1 inoperative, On MISC SYSTEM CTRLS (MSC) maintenance page, confirm right ram fan is operating:
Select "SYS" switch on the Display Select Panel to display the SYS page on an MFD.
Select the MAINT button on the SYS menu.
Select the DISPLAY button for "21 AIR CONDITIONING".
On AIR CONDITIONING maintenance page 2, confirm RAM FAN SPEED KRPM under R is 6.0 or greater.
For VFSG L2 or R2 inoperative, On MISC SYSTEM CTRLS (MSC) maintenance page, confirm left ram fan is operating:
Select "SYS" switch on the Display Select Panel to display the SYS page on an MFD.
Select the MAINT button on the SYS menu.
Select the DISPLAY button for "21 AIR CONDITIONING".
On AIR CONDITIONING maintenance page 2, confirm RAM FAN. SPEED KRPM under L is 6.0 or greater.
ELEC GEN OFF XX advisory message will be displayed while engine is running.
ELEC ENGINE STARTER Lx or Rx may be displayed after engine start.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One starter system on each engine may be inoperative.

Note.Para: For ELEC ENGINE STARTER L1 or R1 status message, right ram air fan motor controller and associated right ram air fan must operate normally (RAM FAN CONTROL R and RAM FAN R are not displayed) for main engine start.

For ELEC ENGINE STARTER L2 or R2 status message, left ram air fan motor controller and associated left ram air fan must operate normally (RAM FAN CONTROL L and RAM FAN L are not displayed) for main engine start.

MaintenanceNote.rev:revHighlight: Added maintenance note to provide further dispatch instruction/clarification.
MaintenanceNote.Para: If the APU is inoperative, Ground Power Engine Start is not allowed if either associated ELEC ENGINE STARTER status message is displayed.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added note # reference only. No content change.

ListItem.Para: Expect engine start time to be longer.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added note to provide further dispatch instruction/clarification.

ListItem.Para: If the APU is inoperative, Ground Power Engine Start is not allowed if either associated ELEC ENGINE STARTER status message is displayed.
